content: We can reframe Self-Refine as a *single-perspective starting point* to build out multi-perspective LLM self-reflection mechanisms: after an initial Self-Refine iteration converges on a polished output from the model's default perspective, we can inject alternative inconsistent solving perspectives to trigger a second round of structured reflection, rather than starting multi-perspective reflection from a messy unrefined initial output. This combined approach retains Self-Refine's core advantage of requiring no extra training or supervised data while resolving the limitation of single-perspective blind spots that plagues naive self-feedback.
